question.text: Under Section 12AA of the PMLA 2002, enhanced due diligence by reporting entities includes which of the following?

answer.text: Verification through Aadhaar, examination of ownership structure, financial position, and source of funds
Verification through PAN only and filing of annual returns with the FIU
Verification through passport and submission of monthly transaction reports to SEBI
Verification through voter ID and mandatory reporting of all transactions above Rs. 50,000 to RBI

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Section 12AA of PMLA 2002 specifies enhanced due diligence measures that include Aadhaar verification, examination of the ownership structure, financial position, and source of funds of the client. This goes beyond standard KYC requirements. **The Trap:** PAN-only verification sounds plausible since PAN is ubiquitous in financial transactions, but enhanced due diligence under Section 12AA specifically requires Aadhaar-based verification along with deeper scrutiny of ownership and fund sources. **Cross-Exam Context:** Enhanced due diligence concepts are also tested in NISM AML/CFT certification and Series X-B exams. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When dealing with high-risk clients or complex ownership structures, always go beyond basic KYC — examine source of funds, beneficial ownership, and financial position as required under enhanced due diligence.
